title: Add ability to build golang remote client
body:
Add the ability to build a remote client in golang that uses all
the same front-end cli code and output code. The initial limitations
here are that it can only be a local client while the bridge and
resolver code is being written for the golang varlink client.

Tests and docs will be added in subsequent PRs.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: @rhatdan @mheon PTAL. Because we wanted to keep this initial PR small, I'm hoping we can merge it reasonably quick so I can look at other subcommands and understand the investment required, make cards, etc

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Doing this reminds me of how gross Info() is under the covers, but that's really not a varlink problem and is completely unrelated to this code. Overall looks fine, one question about where things should go.

> Doing this reminds me of how gross Info() is under the covers, but that's really not a varlink problem and is completely unrelated to this code. Overall looks fine, one question about where things should go.

Yes, I couldnt agree more...but my thought is to get this implemented and fix up info
